text:
Market today: Tech and chip stocks are seeing some positive momentum, with Micron and other chips bouncing back. AI continues to be a key driver, quietly influencing S&P 500 names beyond the usual suspects.

Your holdings: SPAL is a major concern, being a new, leveraged ETF already down almost 50% – this is extremely volatile on margin. SOXL, another leveraged chip ETF, is also deeply in the red with a 'SELL' status. QQQI is on a 'TRIM' signal, though its loss is smaller.

Watch: Keep a close eye on your leveraged positions (SOXL, SPAL) for further decay, especially given the margin exposure. Also, monitor the broader chip sector for sustained recovery.

Bottom line: Prioritize risk management on your leveraged, underperforming positions, as drawdowns are amplified on margin.
plays: SPAL: Consider cutting this new, leveraged position given the nearly 50% loss and high volatility on margin.
SOXL: With a 'SELL' status and deep loss, use any chip bounce (as per headlines) to re-evaluate or reduce exposure.
MSFT: If looking to add, consider the scanner setup for MSFT (entry 397.35-396.95, stop 395.73) as it aligns with AI growth.
